data.description:
# P1.0 — Unify canonical event-byte serialization + zero-dep structural guard

**Prerequisite for P2.1, P2.2, P2.3.** Every later item that adds, reorders, or
signs event content depends on there being exactly ONE canonical byte format.

## Context (problem + research)

Event serialization is scattered across many writers and only **one** is
canonical — `_store/event_append.py:56-61` emits
`json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False)`. Seven
other LIVE writers emit plain `json.dumps(event, ensure_ascii=False)` (unsorted):
reconciler `_engine/event_append.py:123`, `ticket_txn.py:219/351/372`,
`graph/_links.py:145`, `ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py:149`,
`batch_dispatch.py:222`; plus bash heredocs in `ticket-comment.sh:90-91`
(live via `ticket-transition.sh:439` force-close), `ticket-revert.sh:170-174`
(live via `ticket-transition.sh:179` un-archive), `ticket-lib-api.sh:994/1017`
(delete), `ticket-compact.sh:274` (SNAPSHOT). There is **no** byte-parity test —
the one cited in `_store/event_append.py:15`'s docstring does not exist.

**Research / proven art.** The "`json.dumps == jq -S -c`" assumption is unsafe:
`jq` parses numbers as IEEE-754 float64, and rebar's ns `timestamp` is a 19-digit
integer **> 2^53** — jq <=1.6 (default macOS) rounds it on parse, and even jq-1.7
rounds it under arithmetic. The TUF/sigstore ecosystem's hard-won lesson is
"canonicalization across implementations is a footgun; standardize on one / sign
the literal bytes" (RFC 8785 JCS; python-tuf#457; sigstore DSSE). So: collapse to
ONE Python serializer, keep jq out of the event path entirely.

## Evidence (experiments)

- EXP2: `json.dumps(...ensure_ascii=False)` == `jq -S -c` ONLY with
  `ensure_ascii=False` (`世界` diverges otherwise).
- EXP-jq: jq rounds the >2^53 timestamp (`.t+0` -> `...655000`); confirmed.
- EXP-R3 (real code): real `_canonical_bytes` != plain dumps, but parsed dicts are
  identical -> **re-serialization is replay-safe** (reducer reads keys, not bytes).
- EXP-R7 (real code): `python3 -m rebar._store.<submodule>` works and emits
  canonical sorted output -> bash heredocs can call the helper.
- EXP-R9/R9b (real code): the committed stdlib guard
  `docs/experiments/event_write_guard.py` flags **exactly 7 Python + 7 bash** event writers
  with **0 false positives** across 73 read/output `json.dumps` sites.

## What / Implementation detail

1. Factor a **lock-free** `canonical_bytes(event) -> bytes` helper (out of
   `_store/event_append.py:56-61`) importable without the write lock (the txn/LINK
   paths rename+commit inline, e.g. `ticket_txn.py:236-243`).
2. Route every live writer through it: the 5 Python sites import it; the 4 bash
   heredocs call `python3 -m rebar._store.canonical` (pass the event(s) on stdin;
   **batch** multi-event paths — delete writes STATUS+ARCHIVED+N*UNLINK — in ONE
   invocation to avoid per-event subprocess cost).
3. Delete the dead leaf writers (`ticket-create.sh`, `ticket-edit.sh`) and leave
   `ticket-link.sh` (still serves `link --dry-run`, writes no event) /
   `ticket-migrate-*.sh` (one-shot) exempt. Fix the false docstring at
   `_store/event_append.py:15`.
4. Ship the **stdlib** structural guard as a CI test (semgrep/ast-grep/pre-commit
   are ABSENT here — EXP-R9). Python prong = AST (json.dump(s) on a dict carrying
   `event_type`); bash prong = regex scoped to `json.dump` co-occurring with an
   `'event_type'` dict (a bare grep false-positives on ~10 read/output `.sh`).

## Success Criteria

- All event files (every type) are byte-identical regardless of writer, pinned by
  a Python<->Python parity test (incl. a >2^53 int + non-ASCII fuzz case).
- The structural guard fails CI on any new raw event serializer; after this epic
  its expected writer set is **empty** (all routed through the helper).
- No reducer/replay behavior change (existing committed data unaffected;
  `*.retired` bytes are read, not re-serialized).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `canonical_bytes()` extracted as a lock-free helper and imported by the 5 Python writers
- [ ] 4 bash heredoc writers call `python3 -m rebar._store.canonical` (multi-event paths batched)
- [ ] dead leaf writers deleted; `_store/event_append.py:15` docstring corrected
- [ ] `tests/scripts/test-ticket-write-commit-event.sh` added (parity + >2^53/non-ASCII fuzz)
- [ ] stdlib structural guard wired into CI (Python AST + scoped bash regex), expected writer set empty
- [ ] reconciler + compaction suites still green (no replay change)

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.0) for the full write-up and `docs/experiments/` for the prototypes.
data.title: P1.0 — Unify canonical event-byte serialization + zero-dep structural guard
